headings.description: Used for all H1-H4. Delivers an architectural, structural, and professional tone.

body.description: Used for data, labels, and paragraphs. Highly legible, technical, and precise (similar to CAD software interfaces).

mono.description: Used for measurements, dimensions, confidence scores, and raw data display.

colors.strategy: Swiss & High-Contrast (Authority, Clarity, Truth) + Technical SaaS

surfaces.cards: Solid background, 1px subtle inner border (border-border), max 8px border-radius (rounded-md), subtle diffuse shadow. No heavy neumorphism to maintain technical clarity.
surfaces.headers: Crystal Glassmorphism. High opacity (bg-white/70 or bg-black/60), heavy blur (backdrop-blur-xl), 1px bottom border.
surfaces.toolbars: Floating glass panels (Linear style) positioned over the viewer. Heavy blur, high contrast icons.

visual_enhancers.borders: Expose the skeleton. Use 'Grid Borders' concept for data-heavy sections (border-r border-b on grid items) to simulate a blueprint framework.

components.buttons: Sharp or slightly rounded (rounded-md). Solid primary colors for main CTA. Use subtle gray backgrounds with bold text for secondary actions. Always define explicit hover states (lighter hue/transform).
components.statistic_cards: Bento Grid compatible. Large numeric values (IBM Plex Mono). Subtle progress bars or sparklines using the primary color.
components.interactive_viewer: 100% width/height canvas area with crosshair cursor. SVG overlays must use sharp vector rendering. Hovering over a detected object (e.g., Door) brightens its stroke and shows a high-contrast tooltip.
components.file_upload_dropzone: Dashed border with active drag-over state. Inside, use a blueprint or grid pattern texture. Include clear upload progress bars (linear or circular) indicating OpenCV/OCR analysis pipeline steps.
components.toolbars: Floating manual correction toolbar (Edit Mode). Vertical or horizontal pill shape. Tooltips on every icon.

dashboard.rules: Every pixel utilized. Use h-full on children. No jagged bottoms.

marketing.rules: Asymmetry. Leave breathing room. Use spanning (col-span-8, col-span-4).

layouts.homepage: Hero section with large headline, subheadline, and dual CTA (Upload PDF vs View Demo). Asymmetric grid featuring a product mockup image on the right/bottom.
layouts.dashboard_view: Sticky top navbar. Main content area split: Left 75% = Interactive Plan Viewer canvas, Right 25% = Scrollable details panel and Edit Mode toggles. Below canvas = horizontal row of large statistic cards.

motion.interactions: Micro-interactions for feedback. Hover states on cards: transition: transform 0.2s ease, box-shadow 0.2s ease.
motion.page_transitions: Instant or very fast fade (150ms). Speed is a design material.
motion.viewer: Smooth zooming/panning (using framer-motion or d3-zoom). Objects animate in via staggered opacity when analysis is complete.

accessibility.contrast: Minimum 4.5:1 for standard text. Never use pure transparent backgrounds over content. Focus states must have high contrast rings (focus:ring-2 focus:ring-primary).
accessibility.aria: Include aria-labels for all toolbar icons. Tooltips must be accessible.
accessibility.data_testid: ALL interactive and key informational elements MUST include a data-testid attribute in kebab-case, role-based format (e.g., 'upload-dropzone', 'stat-card-total-area', 'tool-add-wall').

icons.library: lucide-react or @phosphor-icons/react (Duotone or Regular for technical feel)
icons.usage: Use crisp, consistent stroke widths. No emojis.

image_urls.description: High-resolution close-up of an architectural floor plan for the hero section or dropzone background.
Architect working on a draft. Good for testimonial or 'Built for professionals' section.

3d_libraries.recommendation: Not strictly required for 2D floor plans, but if layered visualization (e.g., floor layers) is needed, use D3.js or React Spring Parallax for 2.5D separation of detected layers.

instructions_to_main_agent: Prioritize a strict, engineering-grade visual hierarchy. Avoid overly playful shadows or bouncy animations.
Implement Shadcn/UI for foundational components but strip away the default Vercel-look by enforcing the 'Cabinet Grotesk / IBM Plex' typography and 1px technical borders.
Ensure dark mode is a first-class citizen, utilizing slate/obsidian grays, not pure black.
For the plan viewer canvas, use SVG overlays over the uploaded image. Map the specific colors mentioned in the color palette (accent_walls, accent_doors, etc.) to the SVG strokes.
Statistic cards must prominently feature numbers in IBM Plex Mono to emulate technical data readouts.
Include a floating action bar for the 'Edit Mode' (Linear style toolbars).
UNIVERSAL GUIDELINES FOR MAIN AGENT: You tend to converge toward generic, 'on distribution' outputs. Avoid the 'AI slop' aesthetic. Make creative, distinctive frontends.
Dark colors look good when used independently without gradients. DO NOT use dark colors as gradients.
Don't make generic centered layouts. Create depth through layered design elements with z-index hierarchy.
Use glass-morphism effects with backdrop filters (12-24px blur) for sticky navs and toolbars.
You MUST NOT apply universal transition. Eg: transition: all. Always add transitions for specific interactive elements excluding transforms.
You MUST NOT center align the app container. No .App { text-align: center; }.
Always use modern button styles like pill-shaped, or sharp button with interaction animations.
NEVER use AI assistant Emoji characters like 🤖🧠💭💡🔮🎯 etc for icons.
Use 2-3x more spacing than feels comfortable. Cramped designs look cheap.
Add 'data-testid' to ALL interactive elements for E2E testing reliability.
